card_description: PKD is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 38 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick identifies his novel *Ubik* as an accurate depiction of the "unscrambling" of reality into Form I (Life/Real) and Form II (Dead/Hologram)."

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies his novel *Ubik* as an accurate depiction of the "unscrambling" of reality into Form I (Life/Real) and Form II (Dead/Hologram). He posits that Form I (Zebra) is a "supralunar" invader
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_142.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_124.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_131.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_103.txt
